title: The World is your Playground: The new BMW F 450 GS launched in India. - BMW Group
content:
Richa SharmaBMW Group
            

            Tel: +91-124-4566-655



              send an e-mail The World is your Playground: The new BMW F 450 GS launched in India. Iconic GS Design: Full-LED headlight with ‘X’ icon, characteristic
  flyline running from ‘GS’ typical beak right to the slim rear. A GS engineered to conquer: All-new twin-cylinder engine with 48 hp
  power and 43 Nm torque. Calling on all terrains: Strong build, superb off-roading dynamics,
  perfect balance, adjustable sport suspension, excellent ergonomics. Dynamics with comfort: Easy Ride Clutch, Shift Assistant Pro,
  Progressive Damping. Loaded for every journey: Best-in-class 6.5” TFT display with
  multicontroller, heated grips, adjustable hand levers, ABS Pro,
  Dynamic Traction Control, Dynamic Brake Control, Engine Drag Torque
  Control, Riding Modes. Goa. BMW Motorrad India launched the new BMW F 450 GS
  in India today. The highly anticipated motorcycle is available for
  booking throughout the BMW Motorrad India dealer network with
  deliveries starting from June 2026. The BMW F 450 GS has been
  developed in Germany by BMW Motorrad and will be locally produced in India. A GS stands for “best buddy” on and off-road and the BMW F 450 GS
  truly embodies the essence of a GS - get on, twist the throttle, have
  fun - with full control and maximum confidence. Mr. Hardeep Singh Brar, President and CEO, BMW Group India said, “The
  new BMW F 450 GS is built for a new generation of adventure riders, as
  it embarks on a bold new chapter in the GS legacy. Keeping India’s
  diverse roads and riding conditions in mind, this premium offering
  combines iconic GS design, exemplary performance and advanced rider
  technologies to deliver confidence while riding across city streets,
  highways and open trails. Our aim is to inspire and engage even more
  riders to embrace the thrill of adventure and play an active role in
  India’s ever-evolving motorcycle culture. India is a strategic growth
  market for BMW Motorrad, and the F 450 GS is our commitment to
  accelerate this journey, building aspiration, expanding participation
  and enabling every rider to truly make the world their playground.” The introductory ex-showroom prices are as follows: The BMW F 450 GS (Base)                           -          
  INR      4,70,000 The BMW F 450 GS (Exclusive)                    -           INR      4,90,000 The BMW F 450 GS (GS Trophy)                  -           INR      5,30,000 *Prices prevailing at the time of invoicing will be applicable.
  Delivery will be made ex-showroom. Ex-showroom price (inclusive of GST
  and compensation cess) as applicable but excludes Road Tax, RTO
  statutory taxes/fees, other local tax / cess levies and insurance.
  Prices and options are subject to change without prior notice. For
  further information, please contact your local authorized BMW Motorrad Dealer. Thanks to BMW India Financial Services, customized and flexible
  financial solutions are designed as per individual requirements. The
  new BMW F 450 GS can be purchased at a monthly installment starting as
  low as just INR 9,999 per month. Two special integrated financial offers from BMW India Financial
  Services take care of all aspects with a comprehensive ‘all-in’
  package ensuring complete peace of mind and a premium ownership
  experience. The financial offers are designed to simplify and enhance
  the customer purchase journey, centered around a compelling ‘3 Years
  of Care, Your Only Cost is Fuel’ promise. The first offer ‘3 Years of Care’ starts at an EMI of INR 10,499. It
  covers the motorcycle finance, a ‘3-year, unlimited km’ warranty and
  Service Inclusive plan for ‘3 Years, 30,000 km’. The second offer ‘3
  Years of Stylish and Protective Care’ starts at an EMI of INR 12,345.
  It covers the motorcycle finance, a ‘3-year, unlimited km’ warranty,
  Service Inclusive plan for ‘3 Years, 30,000 km’ and selected accessories. The standard warranty can be extended to fourth and fifth year as an
  option. Road-Side Assistance, a 24x7 365 days package further ensures
  prompt support services. The new BMW F 450 GSDesign of the new BMW 450
  GS expresses a rugged yet refined adventure character, blending
  compact proportions with purposeful surfaces. The iconic ‘GS’ design
  philosophy carries itself to the newest member of the GS family. The
  first unmistakable GS design feature in the front look is the
    ‘X’ shaped
full LED headlight. The characteristic flyline runs
  from the front ‘beak’ across to the tank and seat and continues to the
  slim rear. The familiar beak is a mix of black while the side panels
  are in body color. The ‘tooth’ typical of a GS is finished in matte
  Dime Silver metallic. The bike comes with a two-piece seat (845 mm) as
  standard. With its flat, straight design, it provides ideal ergonomic
  conditions for comfort and dynamics for most riders. The body and the
    14-liter tank underneath are designed to provide
  optimal support and a good knee fit when sitting or riding off-road in
  a standing position. The Base and Exclusive variants are available in
  Cosmic Black Color scheme while the GS Trophy variant
  is presented in Racing Blue Metallic with accents in red and
  white. The tank center cover on the GS Trophy variant is in Snaefell
  white and on all other variants it’s matt black. The tank side panel
  is painted in the respective variant color. The BMW F 450 GS comes with lightweight yet robust cast
    aluminum wheels. The bike is fitted with tubeless tires for
  road and off-road use, sized 100/90-19 at the front and 130/80-17 at
  the rear. The front wheel features a floating single-disc
  brake with a Brembo 4-piston fixed caliper and a 310 mm brake
  disc diameter for efficient, stable deceleration. A single disc brake
  with 1-piston floating caliper and 240 mm diameter is used on the rear wheel. The newly developed chassis of the BMW F 450 GS
  relies on a welded tubular steel frame with integrated forged parts.
  This design combines low weight with high robustness, thus meeting the
  demands of both dynamic road riding and demanding off-road passages.
  The front wheel is guided via upside-down telescopic
  forks, while the rear wheel is guided via an aluminum
    hollow cast double-armed swinging arm with a central spring
  strut featuring progressive damping. The stanchions
  are in gold finish, as is the tapered aluminum handlebar. The fork
  bridge is forged. The suspension strut spring features adjustable
  preload and rebound damping. The new BMW F 450 GS is powered by a newly developed two-cylinder
inline engine that boasts an outstanding powertrain
  for dynamic riding pleasure. Its liquid-cooled engine produces a
  maximum output of 48 hp at 8,750 rpm and a maximum torque of
    43 Nm at 6,750 rpm. The lightweight, powerful, high-torque
  and smooth-running engine is installed transversely in the direction
  of travel. Unique to the motorcycle market, it features a crankshaft
  offset by 135 degrees which makes every ride extremely smooth, with
  hardly any vibrations while also providing a thrilling, offbeat
  exhaust note. The newly designed six-speed transmission transmits
  torque to the drive via an X-ring chain. A clear highlight is the
  bike’s clutchless operation, thanks to Shift Assistant
  Pro.It performs smooth, almost imperceptible gear shifts. At
  any time, in any gear, the transmission collaborates perfectly with
  the engine. Easy Ride Clutch (ERC),a segment-first highlight, is
  a further development of a centrifugal clutch which automates starting
  and stopping without any stalling. In combination with Shift Assistant
  Pro, the ERC is a complete game changer. ERC handles low-speed
  engagement, whereas Shift Assistant Pro handles upshifts and
  downshifts, together offering a fully automated-like experience. To ensure pure motorcycling in every scenario, the BMW F 450 GS
  offers four riding modes: 'Rain', 'Road', 'Enduro'
  and ‘Enduro Pro’. The ‘Enduro Pro’ mode is specially designed for
  off-road use with rough terrain tires. Wheelie control and rear wheel
  ABS are deactivated in this mode while DTC and throttle response can
  be customized. A range of standard safety equipment ensures rider
  safety on all adventures. ABS Pro offers increased safety when braking
  in curves by enabling ABS-assisted braking while leaning. Dynamic
  Brake Control offers increased safety when braking, even in difficult
  situations, by preventing unintentional throttle application. The
  Engine Drag Control prevents the rear wheel from locking or skidding
  during abrupt throttle closure or rapid downshifting. The modern digital experience of the BMW F 450 GS focuses on
  convenience, efficiency and supporting the rider with relevant
  information. The best-in-class large 6.5-inch TFT
  display ensures optimum readability, even under difficult
  lighting conditions. The ‘Pure Ride Screen’ and ‘Sport Screen’
  displays are tailored to the BMW GS world and provide detailed
  information on DTC, braking power, and lean angle. The TFT display is
  linked to the multi-controller on the left handlebar, allowing for
  quick and intuitive operation. A USB-C socket in the handlebar area is
  perfect for mobile charging on the ride. A comprehensive range of original BMW Motorrad
  accessories such as protection parts, luggage parts and
  navigation equipments are available for further individualisation of
  the BMW F 450 GS. Cell: +91 99100 22148; Tel: + 91 124 4566600; Email: Richa.Sharma@bmw.in Rohneet Naik, Product and Technology Communication, Press and
  Corporate Affairs Cell: +91 98999 65668; Tel: + 91 124 4566600; Email: Rohneet.NR.Naik@bmw.in YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCCz9St6Kvq2uk-BbaWV15mA Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/bmwmotorrad_IN/ Watch the webcast of the BMW Group Press Conference at the Auto China 2026 in Beijing.

title: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. $GS Shares Sold by Arizona State Retirement System - MarketBeat
content:
Arizona State Retirement System lessened its stake in  The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (NYSE:GS - Free Report) by 5.5% in the fourth quarter, according to the company in its most recent disclosure with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The fund owned 84,678 shares of the investment management company's stock after selling 4,925 shares during the period. Arizona State Retirement System's holdings in The Goldman Sachs Group were worth $74,432,000 at the end of the most recent reporting period. Other institutional investors have also recently added to or reduced their stakes in the company. Dagco Inc. acquired a new stake in  The Goldman Sachs Group during the fourth quarter valued at approximately $25,000.  Harbor Capital Advisors Inc. acquired a new position in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group in the 3rd quarter worth approximately $26,000.  First PREMIER Bank bought a new stake in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group in the 3rd quarter valued at approximately $28,000.  Corundum Trust Company INC bought a new stake in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group in the 3rd quarter valued at approximately $29,000.  Finally, Graney & King LLC raised its position in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group by 51.5% during the 2nd quarter. Graney & King LLC now owns 50 shares of the investment management company's stock valued at $35,000 after acquiring an additional 17 shares in the last quarter. 71.21% of the stock is owned by institutional investors and hedge funds. GS has been the topic of several research analyst reports. BMO Capital Markets raised their price objective on The Goldman Sachs Group from $905.00 to $972.00 and gave the stock a "market perform" rating in a research note on Monday. Morgan Stanley dropped their target price on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $1,078.00 to $1,021.00 and set an "equal weight" rating on the stock in a report on Tuesday, March 31st. Wells Fargo & Company reduced their price target on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $1,050.00 to $1,000.00 and set an "overweight" rating on the stock in a research report on Monday, April 13th. Daiwa Securities Group decreased their price target on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $940.00 to $891.00 and set a "neutral" rating for the company in a report on Tuesday, April 7th. Finally, BNP Paribas Exane boosted their price objective on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $775.00 to $970.00 and gave the company a "neutral" rating in a research report on Friday, January 16th. Eight research analysts have rated the stock with a Buy rating and fourteen have issued  a Hold rating to the company's stock. According to MarketBeat.com, The Goldman Sachs Group  has an average rating of "Hold" and an average target price of $924.14. In other The Goldman Sachs Group news, EVP John F.W. Rogers sold 13,151 shares of the company's stock in a transaction dated Wednesday, February 11th. The shares were sold at an average price of $959.03, for a total value of $12,612,203.53. Following the completion of the sale, the executive vice president directly owned 39,007 shares of the company's stock, valued at approximately $37,408,883.21. This represents a 25.21% decrease in their position. The sale was disclosed in a legal filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which can be accessed through this hyperlink. Also, Treasurer Carey Halio sold 2,846 shares of The Goldman Sachs Group stock in a transaction that occurred on Tuesday, January 27th. The stock was sold at an average price of $927.17, for a total transaction of $2,638,725.82. Following the completion of the transaction, the treasurer directly owned 2,017 shares in the company, valued at $1,870,101.89. This represents a 58.52% decrease in their position. The SEC filing for this sale provides additional information. Over the last three months, insiders have sold 34,003 shares of company stock valued at $32,216,641. Corporate insiders own  0.55% of the company's stock. Shares of NYSE:GS opened at $931.35 on Friday. The company has a 50 day moving average of $868.37 and a 200-day moving average of $861.39. The company has a current ratio of 1.10, a quick ratio of 0.66 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.56. The firm has a market cap of $274.74 billion, a P/E ratio of 17.02, a P/E/G ratio of 1.20 and a beta of 1.32. The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. has a twelve month low of $523.42 and a twelve month high of $984.70. The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS - Get Free Report) last posted its quarterly earnings data on Monday, April 13th. The investment management company reported $17.55 EPS for the quarter, beating analysts' consensus estimates of $15.92 by $1.63. The business had revenue of $17.23 billion for the quarter, compared to analyst estimates of $16.66 billion. The Goldman Sachs Group had a net margin of 14.13% and a return on equity of 16.02%. The business's revenue for the quarter was up 14.4% on a year-over-year basis. During the same quarter last year, the business earned $14.12 earnings per share.  As a group, analysts anticipate that  The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. will post 59.53 earnings per share for the current fiscal year. The company also recently announced a quarterly dividend, which will be paid on Monday, June 29th. Investors of record on Monday, June 1st will be paid a dividend of $4.50 per share. The ex-dividend date of this dividend is Monday, June 1st. This represents a $18.00 annualized dividend and a yield of 1.9%. The Goldman Sachs Group's dividend payout ratio (DPR) is currently 32.89%. Here are the key news stories impacting The Goldman Sachs Group this week: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc is a global investment banking and financial services firm headquartered in New York City. Founded in 1869 as a commercial paper business, the company has grown into a diversified financial institution that provides a broad range of services to corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. The firm is led by Chief Executive Officer David M. Solomon and operates across major financial centers worldwide. Goldman Sachs' core businesses include investment banking, global markets, asset and wealth management, and consumer banking. Want to see what other hedge funds are holding GS?
